     1	package org.ecocean.ia;
     2	
     3	import java.io.IOException;
     4	import java.util.ArrayList;
     5	import java.util.Collection;
     6	import java.util.LinkedHashSet;
     7	import java.util.List;
     8	import java.util.Set;
     9	
    10	import javax.jdo.Query;
    11	
    12	import org.ecocean.shepherd.core.Shepherd;
    13	
    14	/**
    15	 * Direct-SQL utility for the empty-match-prospects batch gate.
    16	 * Returns the set of annotation IDs reachable from a collection of
    17	 * media-asset IDs that pass ml-service match eligibility:
    18	 * {@code matchAgainst=true}, {@code acmId IS NOT NULL}, and (when
    19	 * the matchConfig provides them) have an EMBEDDING row for the
    20	 * configured method/methodVersion.
    21	 *
    22	 * <p>Used by {@link MatchVisibilityGate} (Track 2 C10) to compute
    23	 * the wait set Phase 2 hands to
    24	 * {@link org.ecocean.OpenSearch#waitForAnnotationMatchableIds}.
    25	 * The 4-table join (ANNOTATION + ANNOTATION_FEATURES +
    26	 * MEDIAASSET_FEATURES + EMBEDDING) matches the same shape used at
    27	 * {@code ImportTask.java:781} for repo precedent; direct SQL is
    28	 * preferred over JDOQL here because the join goes through two
    29	 * JDO-generated join tables plus a custom FK table.</p>
    30	 *
    31	 * <p>method/methodVersion match Annotation.getMatchQuery semantics
    32	 * at Annotation.java:1205-1209: each predicate is added only when
    33	 * the value is non-null. Both null skips the EMBEDDING join
    34	 * entirely (legacy api_endpoint-only configs that can't derive a
    35	 * method).</p>
    36	 *
    37	 * <p>(Empty-match-prospects design Track 2 C9.)</p>
    38	 */
    39	public final class MatchEligibilityQuery {
    40	
    41	    private MatchEligibilityQuery() { }
    42	
    43	    /**
    44	     * Resolve the set of match-eligible annotation IDs whose media
    45	     * asset is in {@code siblingMaIds}. The returned set preserves
    46	     * insertion order from the underlying query (which uses
    47	     * {@code SELECT DISTINCT}, so ordering is engine-dependent but
    48	     * stable within a call).
    49	     *
    50	     * @param shep           open Shepherd. Caller manages the
    51	     *                       transaction lifecycle; this method
    52	     *                       neither begins nor commits.
    53	     * @param siblingMaIds   integer MediaAsset IDs from
    54	     *                       {@code topTask.getObjectMediaAssets()}.
    55	     *                       An empty or null collection yields an
    56	     *                       empty result without hitting the DB.
    57	     * @param method         embedding method (e.g. "miewid-msv4.1");
    58	     *                       null skips the predicate.
    59	     * @param methodVersion  embedding method version (e.g. "4.1");
    60	     *                       null skips the predicate.
    61	     * @return distinct annotation IDs, never null.
    62	     * @throws IOException if the underlying JDO/SQL query fails. The
    63	     *         gate translates this to a DEFER outcome with a reason
    64	     *         so an incomplete corpus can't silently green-light
    65	     *         matching (Codex C9 review Major — avoids the v1
    66	     *         "couldn't tell vs no work" ambiguity).
    67	     */
    68	    public static Set<String> findEligibleAnnotationIds(
    69	        Shepherd shep,
    70	        Collection<Integer> siblingMaIds,
    71	        String method,
    72	        String methodVersion)
    73	    throws IOException {
    74	        Set<String> ids = new LinkedHashSet<String>();
    75	        if (siblingMaIds == null || siblingMaIds.isEmpty()) return ids;
    76	
    77	        // Drop nulls / dedupe before building so the same normalized
    78	        // input drives both production execution and test-side shape
    79	        // verification through the shared buildSql.
    80	        LinkedHashSet<Integer> normalized = new LinkedHashSet<Integer>();
    81	        for (Integer id : siblingMaIds) {
    82	            if (id != null) normalized.add(id);
    83	        }
    84	        if (normalized.isEmpty()) return ids;
    85	
    86	        String sql = buildSql(normalized, method, methodVersion);
    87	
    88	        Query q = null;
    89	        try {
    90	            q = shep.getPM().newQuery("javax.jdo.query.SQL", sql);
    91	            @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes")
    92	            List rows = (List) q.execute();
    93	            if (rows != null) {
    94	                for (Object row : rows) {
    95	                    // SELECT-of-one returns the scalar directly, not an
    96	                    // Object[]. Defensive cast on both shapes.
    97	                    String annId = scalarOrFirstColumn(row);
    98	                    if (annId != null) ids.add(annId);
    99	                }
   100	            }
   101	        } catch (Exception ex) {
   102	            // Don't return a quietly-empty set on failure — that would
   103	            // look indistinguishable from "no eligible siblings" and
   104	            // silently green-light matching against an incomplete
   105	            // corpus. Propagate so the gate can DEFER with a reason.
   106	            throw new IOException(
   107	                "MatchEligibilityQuery.findEligibleAnnotationIds() failed: " +
   108	                ex.getMessage(), ex);
   109	        } finally {
   110	            if (q != null) q.closeAll();
   111	        }
   112	        return ids;
   113	    }
   114	
   115	    /**
   116	     * Some JDO query backends return single-column rows as raw
   117	     * scalars, others wrap them in a 1-element {@code Object[]}.
   118	     * Handle both shapes defensively.
   119	     */
   120	    private static String scalarOrFirstColumn(Object row) {
   121	        if (row == null) return null;
   122	        if (row instanceof String) return (String) row;
   123	        if (row instanceof Object[]) {
   124	            Object[] arr = (Object[]) row;
   125	            if (arr.length == 0) return null;
   126	            Object v = arr[0];
   127	            return (v == null) ? null : v.toString();
   128	        }
   129	        return row.toString();
   130	    }
   131	
   132	    /**
   133	     * Escape a SQL string literal for inline interpolation. Method
   134	     * and methodVersion come from admin-controlled IA.json, so SQL
   135	     * injection isn't a typical risk, but this lets the helper hold
   136	     * up if a future call site sources these from less-trusted input.
   137	     */
   138	    private static String sqlEscape(String s) {
   139	        return s.replace("'", "''");
   140	    }
   141	
   142	    /**
   143	     * Shared SQL builder. Called by {@link #findEligibleAnnotationIds}
   144	     * in production AND by the unit tests, so test assertions on the
   145	     * generated SQL match the SQL the database actually executes
   146	     * (Codex C9 review Minor follow-up).
   147	     *
   148	     * <p>Package-visible.</p>
   149	     */
   150	    static String buildSql(Collection<Integer> siblingMaIds, String method,
   151	        String methodVersion) {
   152	        if (siblingMaIds == null) siblingMaIds = new ArrayList<Integer>();
   153	        StringBuilder inList = new StringBuilder();
   154	        int n = 0;
   155	        for (Integer id : siblingMaIds) {
   156	            if (id == null) continue;
   157	            if (n > 0) inList.append(",");
   158	            inList.append(id.intValue());
   159	            n++;
   160	        }
   161	        StringBuilder sql = new StringBuilder();
   162	        sql.append("SELECT DISTINCT a.\"ID\" ");
   163	        sql.append("FROM \"ANNOTATION\" a ");
   164	        sql.append("JOIN \"ANNOTATION_FEATURES\" af ON af.\"ID_OID\" = a.\"ID\" ");
   165	        sql.append("JOIN \"MEDIAASSET_FEATURES\" mf ON mf.\"ID_EID\" = af.\"ID_EID\" ");
   166	        boolean joinEmbedding = method != null || methodVersion != null;
   167	        if (joinEmbedding) {
   168	            sql.append("JOIN \"EMBEDDING\" e ON e.\"ANNOTATION_ID\" = a.\"ID\" ");
   169	        }
   170	        sql.append("WHERE mf.\"ID_OID\" IN (").append(inList).append(") ");
   171	        sql.append("AND a.\"MATCHAGAINST\" = true ");
   172	        sql.append("AND a.\"ACMID\" IS NOT NULL ");
   173	        if (method != null) {
   174	            sql.append("AND e.\"METHOD\" = '").append(sqlEscape(method)).append("' ");
   175	        }
   176	        if (methodVersion != null) {
   177	            sql.append("AND e.\"METHODVERSION\" = '")
   178	                .append(sqlEscape(methodVersion)).append("' ");
   179	        }
   180	        return sql.toString();
   181	    }
   182	}

   448	    // this expects only json passed in, which is to continue paging on results from above
   449	    public JSONObject queryRawScroll(JSONObject scrollData)
   450	    throws IOException {
   451	        if (scrollData == null) throw new IOException("null data passed");
   452	        String scrollId = scrollData.optString("_scroll_id", null);
   453	        if (scrollData == null) throw new IOException("no _scroll_id");
   454	        JSONObject data = new JSONObject();
   455	        data.put("scroll", SEARCH_SCROLL_TIME);
   456	        data.put("scroll_id", scrollId);

   458	        searchRequest.setJsonEntity(data.toString());
   459	        String rtn = getRestResponse(searchRequest);

   461	    }
   462	
   463	    // ml-service migration v2 (commit #7): force pending writes in `indexName`
   464	    // through Lucene's refresh boundary so they are searchable. Synchronous;
   465	    // returns after targeted shards have completed the refresh. NOT a Wildbook
   466	    // queue drain — IndexingManager may still have unindexed entities queued.
   467	    // Callers (typically waitForVisibility) follow with a visibility poll.
   468	    public void indexRefresh(final String indexName)
   469	    throws IOException {
   470	        if (!isValidIndexName(indexName))
   471	            throw new IOException("invalid index name: " + indexName);
   472	        Request req = new Request("POST", indexName + "/_refresh");
   473	        getRestResponse(req);   // discard body; non-2xx surfaces as IOException
   474	    }
   475	
   476	    // ml-service migration v2 (commit #7): bounded poll-and-wait until OpenSearch
   477	    // can see every id in `ids` in `indexName`. Used by MlServiceProcessor
   478	    // (commit #9) post-persist to avoid running findMatchProspects against an
   479	    // index that doesn't yet contain the freshly-written annotations.
   480	    //
   481	    // On entry:
   482	    //   - normalizes `ids` to a Set (drops nulls and duplicates so they can't
   483	    //     prevent the count check from ever succeeding);
   484	    //   - calls _refresh once (synchronous; pushes pending writes through
   485	    //     Lucene's refresh boundary);
   486	    //   - WARNs if /tmp/skipAutoIndexing is set, since that flag will make
   487	    //     every poll return zero hits regardless of how long we wait.
   488	    //
   489	    // Then polls a _count eligibility query with exponential backoff (start
   490	    // 100ms, double, cap 1s) until count >= |normalized ids| OR the total
   491	    // wait reaches timeoutMs. Returns true on visible-success, false on
   492	    // timeout. Caller decides what to do on false (e.g. enqueue a deferred-
   493	    // match job rather than match against a partial index).
   494	    //
   495	    // Does NOT try to drain the Wildbook IndexingManager queue. That queue
   496	    // may contain unrelated entities; queue-depth zero doesn't imply the
   497	    // specific IDs are queryable. Polling visibility IS the correctness gate.
   498	    public boolean waitForVisibility(String indexName, Collection<String> ids,
   499	        long timeoutMs)
   500	    throws IOException {
   501	        if (!isValidIndexName(indexName))
   502	            throw new IOException("invalid index name: " + indexName);
   503	        if (ids == null || ids.isEmpty()) return true;
   504	
   505	        // Normalize: drop nulls + duplicates so the count comparison is
   506	        // against the true number of distinct documents we expect to see.
   507	        Set<String> targetIds = new LinkedHashSet<String>();
   508	        for (String id : ids) {
   509	            if (id != null) targetIds.add(id);
   510	        }
   511	        if (targetIds.isEmpty()) return true;
   512	
   513	        if (skipAutoIndexing()) {
   514	            System.out.println(
   515	                "WARN: OpenSearch.waitForVisibility called with /tmp/skipAutoIndexing set " +
   516	                "— every poll will return zero hits regardless of wait time.");
   517	        }
   518	
   519	        indexRefresh(indexName);
   520	
   521	        JSONObject query = buildIdEligibilityQuery(targetIds);
   522	        long deadline = System.currentTimeMillis() + timeoutMs;
   523	        long sleepMs = 100;
   524	        while (true) {
   525	            int seen = queryCount(indexName, query);
   526	            if (seen >= targetIds.size()) return true;
   527	            long remaining = deadline - System.currentTimeMillis();
   528	            if (remaining <= 0) return false;
   529	            try {
   530	                Thread.sleep(Math.min(sleepMs, remaining));
   531	            } catch (InterruptedException ex) {
   532	                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
   533	                return false;
   534	            }
   535	            sleepMs = Math.min(sleepMs * 2, 1000);
   536	        }
   537	    }
   538	
   539	    // Package-visible for testing. Returns the _count-shaped query body that
   540	    // filters on _id ∈ ids, using OpenSearch's idiomatic `ids` query.
   541	    static JSONObject buildIdEligibilityQuery(Set<String> ids) {
   542	        JSONArray idArr = new JSONArray();
   543	        for (String id : ids) idArr.put(id);
   544	        JSONObject query = new JSONObject();
   545	        query.put("query",
   546	            new JSONObject().put("ids",
   547	                new JSONObject().put("values", idArr)));
   548	        return query;
   549	    }
   550	
   551	    // ml-service migration v2 / empty-match-prospects design Track 2 C8.
   552	    //
   553	    // Stronger visibility predicate than waitForVisibility for the annotation
   554	    // index. A doc that exists by _id but is missing nested
   555	    // embeddings.method/methodVersion would pass _id-only and then knn-fail
   556	    // at match time. This helper polls a predicate that mirrors the matching
   557	    // constraints in Annotation.getMatchQuery: id ∈ ids AND matchAgainst=true
   558	    // AND acmId exists AND a nested embedding for this method/version is
   559	    // indexed. Scope is intentionally narrower than getMatchingSetQuery
   560	    // (no taxonomy/viewpoint/encounter/dead-animal filters) — this helper
   561	    // answers "doc has fresh embedding metadata", which is the visibility
   562	    // race the Track 2 batch gate cares about.
   563	    //
   564	    // method/methodVersion follow the strict-when-present convention of
   565	    // Annotation.getMatchQuery at Annotation.java:1205-1209: if either is
   566	    // null/blank, the corresponding nested predicate is omitted.
   567	    //
   568	    // Like waitForVisibility: _refresh on entry, then exponential-backoff
   569	    // poll of _count until count >= |normalized ids| OR timeout. Empty
   570	    // wait set short-circuits to true.
   571	    public boolean waitForAnnotationMatchableIds(Collection<String> ids,
   572	        String method, String methodVersion, long timeoutMs)
   573	    throws IOException {
   574	        if (ids == null || ids.isEmpty()) return true;
   575	
   576	        Set<String> targetIds = new LinkedHashSet<String>();
   577	        for (String id : ids) {
   578	            if (id != null) targetIds.add(id);
   579	        }
   580	        if (targetIds.isEmpty()) return true;
   581	
   582	        if (skipAutoIndexing()) {
   583	            System.out.println(
   584	                "WARN: OpenSearch.waitForAnnotationMatchableIds called with " +
   585	                "/tmp/skipAutoIndexing set — every poll will return zero hits " +
   586	                "regardless of wait time.");
   587	        }
   588	
   589	        indexRefresh("annotation");
   590	
   591	        JSONObject query = buildAnnotationMatchableQuery(targetIds, method,
   592	            methodVersion);
   593	        long deadline = System.currentTimeMillis() + timeoutMs;
   594	        long sleepMs = 100;
   595	        while (true) {
   596	            int seen = queryCount("annotation", query);
   597	            if (seen >= targetIds.size()) return true;
   598	            long remaining = deadline - System.currentTimeMillis();
   599	            if (remaining <= 0) return false;
   600	            try {
   601	                Thread.sleep(Math.min(sleepMs, remaining));
   602	            } catch (InterruptedException ex) {
   603	                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
   604	                return false;
   605	            }
   606	            sleepMs = Math.min(sleepMs * 2, 1000);
   607	        }
   608	    }
   609	
   610	    // Package-visible for testing. Returns the _count-shaped query body
   611	    // matching the annotation-matchable predicate documented on

   617	        JSONArray idArr = new JSONArray();
   618	        for (String id : ids) idArr.put(id);
   619

   615	    static JSONObject buildAnnotationMatchableQuery(Set<String> ids,
   616	        String method, String methodVersion) {
   617	        JSONArray idArr = new JSONArray();
   618	        for (String id : ids) idArr.put(id);
   619	
   620	        JSONArray filterArr = new JSONArray();
   621	        filterArr.put(new JSONObject().put("ids",
   622	            new JSONObject().put("values", idArr)));
   623	        filterArr.put(new JSONObject().put("term",
   624	            new JSONObject().put("matchAgainst", true)));
   625	        filterArr.put(new JSONObject().put("exists",
   626	            new JSONObject().put("field", "acmId")));
   627	
   628	        // Nested embedding clause. Match Annotation.getMatchQuery at
   629	        // Annotation.java:1205-1209 exactly: omit a predicate only when
   630	        // the value is `null`. A non-null blank string would be a strict
   631	        // term on "" (matching no docs), preserving consistency with the
   632	        // matcher rather than silently broadening the wait predicate.
   633	        // Codex round-1 C8 review surfaced this — empty vs null asymmetry
   634	        // would let the gate green-light docs the matcher then rejects.
   635	        JSONArray nestedMust = new JSONArray();
   636	        if (method != null) {
   637	            nestedMust.put(new JSONObject().put("term",
   638	                new JSONObject().put("embeddings.method", method)));
   639	        }
   640	        if (methodVersion != null) {
   641	            nestedMust.put(new JSONObject().put("term",
   642	                new JSONObject().put("embeddings.methodVersion", methodVersion)));
   643	        }
   644	        JSONObject nestedQuery;
   645	        if (nestedMust.length() == 0) {
   646	            // Both null — wait only on the existence of any nested
   647	            // embedding entry. (Legacy api_endpoint-only configs that
   648	            // can't derive a method.)
   649	            nestedQuery = new JSONObject().put("match_all", new JSONObject());
   650	        } else {
   651	            nestedQuery = new JSONObject().put("bool",
   652	                new JSONObject().put("must", nestedMust));
   653	        }
   654	        filterArr.put(new JSONObject().put("nested",
   655	            new JSONObject().put("path", "embeddings").put("query", nestedQuery)));
   656	
   657	        JSONObject query = new JSONObject();
   658	        query.put("query",
   659	            new JSONObject().put("bool",
   660	                new JSONObject().put("filter", filterArr)));
   661	        return query;
   662	    }

   664	    // when you only care about how many this would return
   665	    public int queryCount(String indexName, final JSONObject query)
   666	    throws IOException {
   667	        if (!isValidIndexName(indexName)) throw new IOException("invalid index name: " + indexName);
   668	        Request searchRequest = new Request("POST", indexName + "/_count");
   669	        JSONObject cleanedQuery = new JSONObject(query.toString());
   670	        cleanedQuery.remove("_source"); // invalid for a _count query
   671	        searchRequest.setJsonEntity(cleanedQuery.toString());
   672	        JSONObject res = new JSONObject();
   673	        try {
   674	            res = new JSONObject(getRestResponse(searchRequest));
   675	        } catch (Exception ex) {
   676	            System.out.println("queryCount() on index " + indexName + " using query=" + query +
   677	                " failed with: " + ex);
   678	            ex.printStackTrace();
   679	            throw new IOException("queryCount() failed");
   680	        }
   681	        return res.optInt("count", -1);
   682	    }

    72	    public long timeInactive() {
    73	        long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
    74	
    75	        if (modified > 0) return (now - modified);
    76	        if (created > 0) return (now - created);
    77	        // weird or inconclusive:
    78	        return -1l;

    81	    public boolean timedOutDueToInactivity() {
    82	        return (timeInactive() > TIMEOUT_INACTIVE_MILLIS);

    85	    public boolean statusInEndState() {
    86	        if ("completed".equals(status)) return true;
    87	        if ("error".equals(status)) return true;
    88	        // ml-service migration v2: "dropped-stale" is terminal — the task's
    89	        // target was deleted before the queued job ran. Neither success nor
    90	        // error; the inactivity-timeout watchdog must not flip it to error.
    91	        if ("dropped-stale".equals(status)) return true;
    92	        return false;
    93	    }

   264	        if (kid == null) return children;
   265	        if (kid.getId().equals(this.getId())) return children; // dont add ourself to children
   266	        if (!children.contains(kid)) children.add(kid);
   267	        return children;

   334	            // Count eligible candidates BEFORE getMatchQuery, which mutates
   335	            // matchingSetQuery in place (Annotation.java:1203). The previous
   336	            // order returned the knn result count instead of the candidate

   338	            // matchingSetQuery to getMatchQuery so the eligible-set object
   339	            // never silently changes shape if a future caller relies on it.
   340	            OpenSearch os = new OpenSearch();
   341	            int numberCandidates = -2;
   342	            try {
   343	                numberCandidates = os.queryCount("annotation", matchingSetQuery);
   344	            } catch (IOException ex) {
   345	                System.out.println("findMatchProspects() numCandidates query failed with " + ex);
   346	            }
   347	            // Read method/version directly from the new _id_conf contract;
   348	            // fall back to splitting model_id for legacy configs.
   349	            String method = iaConfig.optString("method", null);
   350	            String version = iaConfig.optString("version", null);
   351	            if (!Util.stringExists(method)) {
   352	                String[] mv = MLService.getMethodValues(iaConfig);
   353	                method = mv[0];
   354	                version = mv[1];
   355	            }
   356	            JSONObject matchQuery = ann.getMatchQuery(method, version,
   357	                new JSONObject(matchingSetQuery.toString()));
   358	            // i think this will never happen now, due to on-the-fly fix above; but leaving to be safe
   359	            if (matchQuery == null) {
   360	                System.out.println("findMatchProspects() cannot getMatches() on " + ann +
   361	                    " due to no suitable embeddings for " + iaConfig);
   362	                subTask.setStatus("error");
   363	                subTask.setStatusDetailsAddError("REQUIRED",
   364	                    "no suitable embeddings for getMatches()");
   365	                subTask.setCompletionDateInMilliseconds();
   366	                myShepherd.getPM().makePersistent(subTask);
   367	                subtasksFailed++;
   368	                continue; // on to next ann
   369	            }
   370	            List<Annotation> prospects = ann.getMatches(myShepherd, matchQuery);
   371	            System.out.println("findMatchProspects() on " + ann + " found " +
   372	                Util.collectionSize(prospects) + " prospects (in " + numberCandidates +
   373	                " candidates) for subTask " + subTask.getId());
   374	            boolean mrOk = true;
   375	            try {
   376	                // we build this even if empty, cuz that means we got results; just not nice ones
   377	                MatchResult mr = new MatchResult(subTask, prospects, numberCandidates, myShepherd);
   378	                System.out.println("findMatchProspects() created " + mr + " on " + subTask);
   379	                myShepherd.getPM().makePersistent(mr);
   380	            } catch (IOException ex) {
   381	                System.out.println("findMatchProspects() MatchResult creation failed on " +
   382	                    subTask + ": " + ex);
   383	                ex.printStackTrace();
   384	                mrOk = false;
   385	            }
   386	            if (mrOk) {
   387	                subTask.setStatus("completed");
   388	                subtasksOk++;
   389	            } else {
   390	                subTask.setStatus("error");
   391	                subTask.setStatusDetailsAddError("UNKNOWN",
   392	                    "MatchResult persistence failed");
   393	                subtasksFailed++;
   394	            }
   395	            subTask.setCompletionDateInMilliseconds();
   396	            myShepherd.getPM().makePersistent(subTask);
   397	        }
   398	        // Reflect subtask outcomes on the parent. Any failure marks the
   399	        // parent "error" with a PARTIAL detail; otherwise "completed".
   400	        // No "partial" terminal status is introduced — Task.statusInEndState
   401	        // only recognizes completed/error, so adding one would ripple into
   402	        // polling/UI semantics.
   403	        if (subtasksFailed == 0) {
   404	            task.setStatus("completed");
   405	        } else {
   406	            task.setStatus("error");
   407	            task.setStatusDetailsAddError("PARTIAL",
   408	                subtasksFailed + " of " + (subtasksOk + subtasksFailed) + " subtasks failed");
   409	        }
   410	        task.setCompletionDateInMilliseconds();

   362	    private JSONObject sendPayload(String endpoint, JSONObject payload)
   363	    throws IAException {
   364	        if (endpoint == null) throw new IAException("null api_endpoint");
   365	        URL url = null;
   366	        try {
   367	            url = new URL(endpoint);
   368	        } catch (MalformedURLException urlEx) {
   369	            throw new IAException("api_endpoint url error: " + urlEx);
   370	        }
   371	        try {
   372	            // throws IOException, java.net.ProtocolException
   373	            JSONObject res = RestClient.postJSON(url, payload, null);
   374	            return res;
   375	        } catch (Exception ex) {
   376	            System.out.println("sendPayload(" + url + ") threw " + ex);
   377	            ex.printStackTrace();
   378	            String msg = ex.getMessage();
   379	            if (msg == null) msg = ""; // safety against NPE
   380	            if (msg.contains("Connection refused")) {
   381	                throw new IAException("Connection refused", true, true);
   382	            } else if (msg.contains("Read timed out")) {
   383	                throw new IAException("time out", true); // no increment
   384	            } else if (msg.contains("HTTP error code : 500")) {
   385	                throw new IAException("500 error", true, true);
   386	            } else if (msg.contains("HTTP error code : 502")) {
   387	                throw new IAException("502 error", true); // we requeue, but dont increment this?
   388	            }
   389	            // default behavior is to retry, but with increment
   390	            throw new IAException("unhandled exception [will requeue, incremented] on POST: " + ex,

  1191	    // this version if you already have matchingSetQuery

  1193	        JSONObject matchingSetQuery) {
  1194	        Embedding emb = getEmbeddingByMethod(method, methodVersion);
  1195	
  1196	        if (emb == null) return null;
  1197	        JSONObject nested = new JSONObject(
  1198	            "{\"nested\": {\"path\": \"embeddings\", \"query\": {\"bool\": {}}}}");
  1199	        JSONArray must = new JSONArray();

  1207	        if (methodVersion != null)
  1208	            must.put(new JSONObject("{\"term\": {\"embeddings.methodVersion\":\"" + methodVersion +
  1209	                "\"}}"));
  1210	        nested.getJSONObject("nested").getJSONObject("query").getJSONObject("bool").put("must",
  1211	            must);
  1212	
  1213	        // we put nested under its own top-level must, that way its score counts (whereas filter does not)
  1214	        JSONArray nestedMust = new JSONArray();
  1215	        nestedMust.put(nested);
  1216	        matchingSetQuery.getJSONObject("query").getJSONObject("bool").put("must", nestedMust);
  1217	        return matchingSetQuery;
  1218	    }

   265	    public static boolean findMatchProspects(JSONObject iaConfig, Task task, Shepherd myShepherd) {
   266	        // Migration plan v2 §commit #3: gate accepts the new _id_conf

   277	            task.setStatus("completed");
   278	            task.setCompletionDateInMilliseconds();
   279	            return true;
   280	        }
   281	        System.out.println("findMatchProspects() (task " + task.getId() + ", " +
   282	            task.numberAnnotations() + " annots) has embedding match: " + iaConfig);

   284	        // reflects reality (plan v2 §commit #3: previous code unconditionally
   285	        // marked the parent "completed" even if every subtask failed).

  2045	
  2046	    // suppose we could order by created?
  2047	    public Embedding getEmbeddingByMethod(String method, String methodVersion) {
  2048	        if (numberEmbeddings() < 1) return null;
  2049	        Iterator it = embeddings.iterator();
  2050	        if (method == null) return (Embedding)it.next();
  2051	        while (it.hasNext()) {
  2052	            Embedding emb = (Embedding)it.next();
  2053	            if (!method.equals(emb.getMethod())) continue;
  2054	            if ((methodVersion == null) || (methodVersion.equals(emb.getMethodVersion())))
  2055	                return emb;
  2056	        }
  2057	        return null;
  2058	    }
  2059	
  2060	    // this will match only vector (not other properties)
  2061	    public Embedding findEmbeddingByVector(Embedding find) {
  2062	        if (find == null) return null;
  2063	        if (numberEmbeddings() < 1) return null;
  2064	        Iterator it = embeddings.iterator();
  2065	        while (it.hasNext()) {
  2066	            Embedding emb = (Embedding)it.next();
  2067	            if (emb.hasEqualVector(find)) return emb;
  2068	        }
  2069	        return null;
  2070	    }

 succeeded in 239ms:
     1	# Design: fix empty match prospects on bulk imports (revision 6, LOCKED post-Codex round 5)
     2	
     3	## Context
     4	
     5	On amphibian-reptile.wildbook.org (ml-service v2 live test deployment),
     6	bulk-imported fire salamanders return 0–5 match prospects per
     7	annotation even when the import contains 7+ same-species annotations
     8	that should match each other. Manual OpenSearch queries a few minutes
     9	later return all the expected hits, so the data lands correctly — but
    10	at match-task time the candidate pool is incomplete.
    11	
    12	This design fixes two linked bugs that together produce the empty
    13	results. Either fix on its own leaves a residual.
    14	
    15	The two bugs:
    16	
    17	1. **WBIA image-then-annotation registration** — the v2 routing path
    18	   skips legacy `WBIA.sendMediaAssets()`, so the annotation-registration
    19	   polling thread fires `/api/annot/json/` against WBIA without WBIA
    20	   knowing about the image. Returns HTTP 500
    21	   `image_uuid_list has invalid values [(0, None)]`. (See existing
    22	   paused design at `2026-05-18-wbia-image-registration-design.md`.)
    23	2. **Match runs before sibling ml-service jobs complete and before
    24	   their annotations are visible in OpenSearch.** Each per-image
    25	   ml-service job calls `MlServiceProcessor.waitAndRunMatch` as soon
    26	   as *it* persists detections; siblings may still be mid-HTTP or
    27	   pre-persist. Even after they persist, OS indexing is async with
    28	   its own refresh interval. The match for any one annotation runs
    29	   against a candidate pool that doesn't yet include the rest of
    30	   the batch.
    31	
    32	## Revision history
    33	
    34	**v1 → v2** (post-Codex round 1; 2 blockers, 3 majors, 1 medium):
    35	two-phase gate (MA terminal-state + OS visibility), topTask
    36	boundary, eligibility filter, testability seam, deferred-match
    37	re-gating with attempt counter.
    38	
    39	**v5 → v6 (LOCKED)** (post-Codex round 5; 1 blocker + 1 major
    40	only, with Codex confirming all v4→v5 decisions and the core
    41	Track 2 shape):
    42	
    43	- **Deferred-match routing flags fixed** (Codex round-5 Blocker).
    44	  Payload now includes `mlServiceV2: true` AND `deferredMatch:
    45	  true` (the consumers used by `IAGateway.java:637` and
    46	  `MlServiceProcessor.java:55`). `mlServiceV2DeferredMatch` stays
    47	  as an extra diagnostic marker, not the routing contract.
    48	- **`method == null` handled alongside `methodVersion == null`**
    49	  (Codex round-5 Major). Both predicates conditionally omitted in
    50	  Phase 2 SQL and in `waitForAnnotationMatchableIds`, matching
    51	  `Annotation.getMatchQuery`'s independent strict-when-present
    52	  behavior at `Annotation.java:1205-1209`.
    53	- Codex round-5 explicitly confirmed: `safeInvalidate` placement
    54	  on `QueryCacheFactory`, `MatchEligibilityQuery` extraction,
    55	  topTask-null WARN, the two-wait split, the raw status accessor,
    56	  and that the SQL join shape matches existing repo precedent at
    57	  `ImportTask.java:781`. **Design locked.**
    58	
    59	**v4 → v5** (post-Codex round 4; 1 blocker + 1 blocker/major + 3
    60	majors + 2 mediums; Codex signaled lock after these):
    61	
    62	- **Deferred enqueue explicitly calls `IAGateway.requeueJob(payload, true)`**
    63	  (Codex round-4 Blocker). Pseudocode rewritten to call the
    64	  function by name; `__queueRetries` is set by `requeueJob`
    65	  itself, not by us.
    66	- **`MediaAsset.getId()` comparison uses `.equals()`** (Codex
    67	  round-4 Blocker/Major). `getId()` returns `String`, not `int`;
    68	  `==` is reference equality.
    69	- **Wait set is split into two predicates** (Codex round-4
    70	  Major). Caller IDs wait on `_id` visibility (existing
    71	  `waitForVisibility`); sibling eligible IDs wait on
    72	  `waitForAnnotationMatchableIds` (new matchable predicate).
    73	  Prevents a caller annotation that's not-yet-matchable from
    74	  blocking its own match until age-out.
    75	- **Phase 2 SQL skips `methodVersion` predicate when null**
    76	  (Codex round-4 Major), matching `Annotation.getMatchQuery` at
    77	  `Annotation.java:1207`. Method/version derived using the same
    78	  fallback chain as `Embedding.findMatchProspects` —
    79	  `iaConfig.method`/`version` then `MLService.getMethodValues`
    80	  for legacy configs.
    81	- **`Task.getStoredStatus()` added** to `Task.java` (Codex round-4
    82	  Major). Returns the raw persisted status field without the
    83	  timed-out-task mutation side-effect of `getStatus(Shepherd)`.
    84	- **`MatchEligibilityQuery` extracted as package-visible class
    85	  in `org.ecocean.ia`** (Codex round-4 OQ #2). Schema-sensitive
    86	  SQL deserves focused tests; not buried inside the gate impl.
    87	- **`safeInvalidate(context, name)` lives on `QueryCacheFactory`**
    88	  (Codex round-4 OQ #1).
    89	- **`topTask == null` logs WARN with childTaskId** (Codex round-4
    90	  OQ #3). Abnormal degraded path, not silent.
    91	- **Stale `MAX_DEFER_ATTEMPTS` / 50min references removed**
    92	  (Codex round-4 Medium).
    93	- **`MlServiceProcessor` gets a package-visible
    94	  `DeferredMatchPublisher` seam** (Codex round-4 Medium) so DEFER
    95	  assertions are clean without exposing private internals.
    96	
    97	**v3 → v4** (post-Codex round 3; 1 blocker + 3 majors + 3 mediums):
    98	
    99	- **`MAX_DEFER_AGE_MILLIS` reduced to 12 minutes** (Codex round-3
   100	  Blocker). `IAGateway.requeueJob` caps at `MAX_RETRIES=30` with
   101	  30s sleep (`IAGateway.java:751-810`) — ~15 min before the queue
   102	  itself gives up. 12 min keeps `GIVE_UP` reachable inside the
   103	  real cap with a small margin. Design no longer assumes any new
   104	  scheduler.
   105	- **Sibling child-task lookup uses `topTask.getChildren()` directly**
   106	  (Codex round-3 Major). `Task.getTasksFor(ma)` returns any task
   107	  containing the MA (includes topTask itself and old unrelated
   108	  tasks) — wrong API. The gate iterates `topTask.getChildren()`
   109	  and picks the child whose singleton `objectMediaAssets` contains
   110	  the sibling MA.
   111	- **Child Task terminal-status set corrected** (Codex round-3
   112	  Major). `Task.statusInEndState()` at `Task.java:85` recognizes
   113	  `"completed"`, `"error"`, `"dropped-stale"` for child tasks
   114	  (note the distinction: `"completed"` for Task vs `"complete"`
   115	  for MA detectionStatus). Use a raw persisted-status accessor,
   116	  NOT `Task.getStatus(Shepherd)` which can mutate timed-out
   117	  tasks (`Task.java:597`).
   118	- **`waitForMatchable` renamed and rescoped** (Codex round-3
   119	  Major). Renamed to `waitForAnnotationMatchableIds` (per round-3
   120	  open-question answer #3); scope explicitly limited to "doc has
   121	  fresh embedding metadata" (id + matchAgainst + acmId + nested
   122	  embeddings.method/methodVersion). NOT a full matchingSetQuery
   123	  replication — the narrower semantics are sufficient for the
   124	  index-visibility race we're solving.
   125	- **`_count` body shape** uses `ids` query (matching existing
   126	  `OpenSearch.queryCount` at `OpenSearch.java:541`), no `size`,
   127	  no `track_total_hits` (Codex round-3 Medium).
   128	- **Phase 2 prose** updated to reference both `STATUS_COMPLETE`
   129	  and `STATUS_COMPLETE_MLSERVICE` where it previously said only
   130	  `complete` (Codex round-3 Medium).
   131	- **Cache invalidation null-safety** (Codex round-3 Medium): wrap
   132	  `QueryCacheFactory.getQueryCache(context).invalidateByName(name)`
   133	  in a small `safeInvalidate(context, name)` helper because the
   134	  factory can return null on uninitialized contexts.
   135	
   136	**v2 → v3** (post-Codex round 2; 1 blocker + 1 blocker/major + 3
   137	majors + 1 medium):
   138	
   139	- **Gate API takes `callerAnnotationIds`** (Codex round-2 Blocker).
   140	  Pseudocode and behavioral spec aligned.
   141	- **Terminal-status table replaced with the actual v2 constants**
   142	  from `IBEISIA.java:73-80` (Codex round-2 Blocker/Major). Includes
   143	  `STATUS_COMPLETE_MLSERVICE`, `STATUS_PROCESSING_MLSERVICE`,
   144	  `STATUS_PENDING_SPECIES`, `STATUS_DROPPED_STALE` that v2 missed.
   145	  Phase 1 now considers **both** `MediaAsset.detectionStatus` AND
   146	  the per-MA child Task status; either being terminal-error short-
   147	  circuits to "no annotations expected from this sibling".
   148	- **Matchable-visibility predicate**, not just `_id` count (Codex
   149	  round-2 Major). Phase 3 polls with the matchingSetQuery's
   150	  eligibility predicate plus `_id` filter, so a stale doc indexed
   151	  by id but missing nested `embeddings.method`/`methodVersion`
   152	  cannot pass the gate.
   153	- **Shepherd boundary spelled out** in `MatchVisibilityGateImpl`
   154	  (Codex round-2 Major): Phase 1 + 2 run under a single Shepherd
   155	  scope which **closes** before Phase 3's OpenSearch poll. No
   156	  transaction held across the visibility wait.
   157	- **Deferred backoff defined explicitly** (Codex round-2 Major):
   158	  reuse existing `IAGateway.requeueJob` 30s fixed delay (verified
   159	  at `IAGateway.java:785`); add `firstDeferredAt` epoch-ms in
   160	  jobData for elapsed-time age-out; cap by elapsed time (60min)
   161	  rather than attempt count alone.
   162	- **Cache names corrected** (Codex round-2 Medium): existing
   163	  `iaAnnotationIdsStrict` reuses cache key `"iaAnnotationIds"`
   164	  (`WildbookIAM.java:478`); new strict-image variant follows the
   165	  same pattern and shares key `"iaImageIds"`. Invalidations target
   166	  the shared key. The WBIA doc's stale `invalidate("iaImageIds")`
   167	  text was correct; the v2 revision's `iaImageIdsStrict` cache
   168	  key was wrong.
   169	
   170	## Goal
   171	
   172	Restore the user-expected behavior: when I upload N same-species
   173	photos and the import completes, the per-annotation match results
   174	include the other N-1 annotations from my upload (when they match
   175	visually) plus any pre-existing candidates from the corpus. WBIA
   176	stays in sync as a HotSpotter fallback.
   177	
   178	## Non-goals
   179	
   180	- Detection-time WBIA image registration. Same rationale as the
   181	  paused WBIA design — handling retroactively in the polling
   182	  thread keeps the intake fast path simple.
   183	- Restructuring ml-service to batch detection. Per-image jobs are
   184	  the natural granularity for v2; batching belongs at the match
   185	  invocation seam, not detection.
   186	- Moving away from `waitForVisibility`. It works correctly — its
   187	  call-site scope and pre-conditions are the bug, not the helper.
   188	- A barrier task type / new dispatcher branch. We solve this at the
   189	  existing `waitAndRunMatch` seam plus a small collaborator.
   190	
   191	## Audit: what already exists
   192	
   193	| Helper | File:line | Status |
   194	|---|---|---|
   195	| `OpenSearch.waitForVisibility(indexName, ids, timeoutMs)` | `OpenSearch.java:498` | Works. `_refresh` + bounded `_count` poll. |
   196	| `OpenSearch.indexRefresh(indexName)` | `OpenSearch.java:468` | Synchronous refresh helper. |
   197	| `MlServiceProcessor.waitAndRunMatch(annotationIds, taskId, matchConfig)` | `MlServiceProcessor.java:418` | Per-job wrapper; current implementation widens nothing. Falls back to `enqueueDeferredMatch` on timeout. |
   198	| `MlServiceProcessor.runMatchProspects(annotationIds, taskId, matchConfig)` | `MlServiceProcessor.java:444` | Builds match Task, attaches annotations, calls `Embedding.findMatchProspects`. |
   199	| `MlServiceProcessor.runDeferredMatch(jobData)` | `MlServiceProcessor.java:433` | Today: direct `runMatchProspects`. Bug: no re-gate. |
   200	| `MlServiceProcessor.enqueueDeferredMatch(annotationIds, parentTaskId)` | `MlServiceProcessor.java:681` | Re-queues via IAGateway with `mlServiceV2DeferredMatch: true`. Today: no attempt counter. |
   201	| `IA.enqueueOneAssetForMlService` creates per-asset `Task childTask = new Task(topTask)` | `IA.java:281-287` | Per-asset child has parent=topTask; topTask owns `objectMediaAssets` (the same-species batch). |
   202	| `MediaAsset.getDetectionStatus()` | per JDO | Lifecycle: `_new` → `_post_new` → `processing` → `complete` \| `error` \| `pending`. |
   203	| `Embedding.findMatchProspects(iaConfig, task, shepherd)` | `Embedding.java:265` | Per-annotation knn + matchingSetQuery on `task.getObjectAnnotations()`. |
   204	| `QueryCache.invalidateByName(name)` | `QueryCache.java:46` | Right API for cache invalidation (not "invalidate"). |
   205	| `WildbookIAM` helpers — `iaImageIds`, `sendMediaAssetsForceId`, `mediaAssetToUri`, `validMediaAsset` | `WildbookIAM.java` | See existing WBIA design doc. |
   206	
   207	Note: `findMatchProspects` already loops `task.getObjectAnnotations()`
   208	correctly — the matching itself is fine. The bug is the **gate** in
   209	front of it.
   210	
   211	## Design
   212	
   213	### Track 1 — WBIA image-then-annotation registration
   214	
   215	No structural change from
   216	`docs/plans/2026-05-18-wbia-image-registration-design.md`. Codex
   217	follow-ups to address before implementation:
   218	
   219	1. `mediaAssetToUri(MediaAsset)` is `private`. Promote to
   220	   `public static String` (returning `String`, tightening
   221	   `ma.webURL()` null-check).
   222	2. New strict variant `iaImageIdsStrict` with 15-min QueryCache
   223	   (matching `iaAnnotationIdsStrict` from c11 fix-pass). Lenient
   224	   `iaImageIds` stays cache-free for backward compatibility.
   225	3. Phase A eligibility check gains `ma.isValidImageForIA() != null
   226	   && !ma.isValidImageForIA()` AND `WildbookIAM.validMediaAsset(ma)`
   227	   (in the order `sendMediaAssetsForceId` uses them at
   228	   `WildbookIAM.java:121-130`). If either fails, park at
   229	   `MAX_ATTEMPTS`.
   230	4. Phase B does NOT re-validate MA eligibility. Documented as an
   231	   explicit decision: MA validity does not change between Phase A
   232	   commit and Phase B HTTP. One-line comment in code.
   233	5. `parseImageIdsArrayStrict` is added alongside
   234	   `parseAnnotationIdsArrayStrict`. A shared
   235	   `parseFancyUuidListStrict(jids, ctxLabel)` helper extracts the
   236	   commonality. Both go through `fromFancyUUID`.
   237	6. No new outcome enum value. Image-registration failures reuse
   238	   the existing `NETWORK_FAIL` outcome; Phase C log line
   239	   distinguishes phase via existing log string (Phase 0 / 1 / 2).
   240	7. `WbiaRegisterRequest` gains four image-side fields, captured in
   241	   Phase A:
   242	
   243	```java
   244	public final String imageUri;
   245	public final Double imageLatitude;
   246	public final Double imageLongitude;
   247	public final Long imageDateTimeMillis;
   248	```
   249	
   250	`mediaAssetUuid` is not a separate field —
   251	`MediaAsset.acmId == MediaAsset.uuid` (commit `2a3eab63a`), so
   252	`dto.mediaAssetAcmId` already carries the value
   253	`sendMediaAssetsForceId` puts in `image_uuid_list`.
   254	
   255	#### Cache-name fix (Codex round-2 Medium)
   256	
   257	The existing `iaAnnotationIdsStrict` method reuses the lenient
   258	variant's cache key `"iaAnnotationIds"` (see `WildbookIAM.java:478`,
   259	verified). Strict and lenient share a single cache; the strict
   260	variant just adds error-raising semantics on top.

exec
/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba docs/plans/2026-05-18-empty-match-prospects-design.md | sed -n '314,520p;580,725p;760,830p;960,1018p'" in /mnt/c/Wildbook-clean2
 succeeded in 253ms:
   314	#### Phase 1: sibling terminal-state gate
   315	
   316	For each sibling MA in `topTask.getObjectMediaAssets()`, evaluate
   317	**both** `ma.getDetectionStatus()` AND the per-MA child Task's
   318	**raw persisted status**.
   319	
   320	**Finding the per-MA child Task** (Codex round-3 Major): do NOT
   321	use `Task.getTasksFor(ma)` — it returns ANY task containing that
   322	MA, which includes the topTask itself (whose
   323	`objectMediaAssets` contains all siblings) and possibly older
   324	unrelated tasks. Instead iterate `topTask.getChildren()` and
   325	select the child whose singleton `objectMediaAssets` contains
   326	the sibling MA:
   327	
   328	```java
   329	private Task findChildTaskForSibling(Task topTask, MediaAsset siblingMa) {
   330	    List<Task> children = topTask.getChildren();
   331	    if (children == null) return null;
   332	    for (Task child : children) {
   333	        List<MediaAsset> mas = child.getObjectMediaAssets();
   334	        if (mas != null && mas.size() == 1 &&
   335	            siblingMa.getId().equals(mas.get(0).getId())) {
   336	            return child;
   337	        }
   338	    }
   339	    return null;
   340	}
   341	```
   342	
   343	**Use raw persisted status** (Codex round-3 Major + round-4
   344	Major). The current public getter `Task.getStatus(Shepherd)` is
   345	derived and can mutate timed-out tasks (`Task.java:597`), which we
   346	don't want inside the gate. A raw accessor does NOT exist yet, so
   347	this design adds one:
   348	
   349	```java
   350	// In Task.java, alongside getStatus():
   351	/**
   352	 * Returns the persisted status field WITHOUT the timed-out-task
   353	 * mutation side-effect that getStatus(Shepherd) performs. Use
   354	 * this when reading status for read-only decisions (e.g., gating)
   355	 * where mutating timed-out tasks as a side-effect would corrupt
   356	 * the caller's logic.
   357	 */
   358	public String getStoredStatus() {
   359	    return this.status;
   360	}
   361	```
   362	
   363	The gate reads `siblingChildTask.getStoredStatus()` and tests
   364	against the terminal set listed below. Either-being-terminal is
   365	sufficient.
   366	
   367	Terminal vs non-terminal status sets:
   368	
   369	**MediaAsset.detectionStatus** (constants from `IBEISIA.java:73-82`).
   370	Terminal:
   371	- `STATUS_COMPLETE` = `"complete"` (legacy WBIA path success)
   372	- `STATUS_COMPLETE_MLSERVICE` = `"complete-mlservice"` (v2 success)
   373	- `STATUS_PENDING` = `"pending"` (human review; won't auto-progress)
   374	- `STATUS_PENDING_SPECIES` = `"pending-species"` (no taxonomy)
   375	- `STATUS_ERROR` = `"error"`
   376	- `STATUS_DROPPED_STALE` = `"dropped-stale"`
   377	
   378	Non-terminal:
   379	- `STATUS_INITIATED` = `"initiated"`
   380	- `STATUS_PROCESSING` = `"processing"`
   381	- `STATUS_PROCESSING_MLSERVICE` = `"processing-mlservice"`
   382	- `"_new"` / `"_post_new"` (MA lifecycle pre-processing)
   383	- `null` (uninitialized)
   384	
   385	**Task.status** (per `Task.statusInEndState()` at `Task.java:85`,
   386	which is the source of truth for the per-MA child Task; note the
   387	"completed" / "complete" distinction between Task and MA).
   388	Terminal:
   389	- `"completed"` (child Task success; set by `markTaskCompleted`
   390	  at `MlServiceProcessor.java:668`)
   391	- `"error"`
   392	- `"dropped-stale"`
   393	
   394	Non-terminal:
   395	- `null`, `""`, `"queuing"`, `"working"`, or any other value.
   396	
   397	A sibling is treated as **terminal** for the gate when
   398	`isMaTerminal(ma.detectionStatus) OR isTaskTerminal(childTask.rawStatus)`.
   399	
   400	If any sibling is non-terminal, **defer** (see deferred-path
   401	section). Caller's match does NOT run yet.
   402	
   403	If all siblings are terminal, proceed to Phase 2.
   404	
   405	#### Phase 2: collect eligible annotation IDs
   406	
   407	For each sibling MA in topTask whose detectionStatus is in
   408	`{STATUS_COMPLETE, STATUS_COMPLETE_MLSERVICE}` (i.e., contributed
   409	annotations successfully), enumerate annotations through
   410	`MediaAsset → Feature → Annotation`, filtered for ml-service match
   411	eligibility. Siblings with terminal-error states
   412	(`STATUS_ERROR`, `STATUS_DROPPED_STALE`) contributed nothing — they
   413	are skipped from the eligibility set, not waited for.
   414	
   415	**Method/version derivation** (Codex round-4 Major): use the same
   416	fallback chain as `Embedding.findMatchProspects`
   417	(`Embedding.java:349-355`). Read `iaConfig.method` and
   418	`iaConfig.version` first; if `method` is blank, fall back to
   419	`MLService.getMethodValues(iaConfig)[0]` and `[1]`. Carry both
   420	values into Phase 2's SQL.
   421	
   422	**Encapsulation** (Codex round-4 OQ #2): the SQL lives in a new
   423	package-visible `MatchEligibilityQuery` class under
   424	`org.ecocean.ia` so it has its own test surface, not buried
   425	inside `MatchVisibilityGateImpl`:
   426	
   427	```java
   428	package org.ecocean.ia;
   429	
   430	public final class MatchEligibilityQuery {
   431	    public static Set<String> findEligibleAnnotationIds(
   432	        Shepherd shep,
   433	        Collection<String> siblingMaIds,
   434	        String method,
   435	        String methodVersion)
   436	    throws SQLException;
   437	}
   438	```
   439	
   440	```sql
   441	-- Sketch; JDOQL equivalent in code.
   442	SELECT DISTINCT a."ID"
   443	FROM "ANNOTATION" a
   444	JOIN "ANNOTATION_FEATURES" af ON af."ID_OID" = a."ID"
   445	JOIN "MEDIAASSET_FEATURES" mf ON mf."ID_EID" = af."ID_EID"
   446	JOIN "MEDIAASSET" ma ON ma."ID" = mf."ID_OID"
   447	JOIN "EMBEDDING" e ON e."ANNOTATION_ID" = a."ID"
   448	WHERE ma."ID" IN (<topTask sibling MA ids>)
   449	  AND a."MATCHAGAINST" = true
   450	  AND a."ACMID" IS NOT NULL
   451	  AND e."METHOD" = ?
   452	  AND e."METHODVERSION" = ?
   453	```
   454	
   455	The `EMBEDDING JOIN ... METHOD = ?` clause is the key filter — it
   456	ensures we only wait for annotations that actually have an
   457	embedding of the model we're matching against. This excludes:
   458	- Bulk-import placeholder annotations created by `BulkImporter`
   459	  (no embedding at all, never indexed in OS due to
   460	  `skipAutoIndexing`).
   461	- Legacy annotations from a different embedding method.
   462	- Annotations whose ml-service extraction failed
   463	  (`predictModelId` set but `EMBEDDING` row never created).
   464	
   465	**`method`/`methodVersion` null handling** (Codex round-4 Major +
   466	round-5 Major). Both fields are independently nullable for legacy
   467	`api_endpoint`-only configs that `Embedding.findMatchProspects`
   468	accepts as vector configs (`Embedding.java:269`), where
   469	`MLService.getMethodValues` can return null in either slot
   470	(`MLService.java:348`). `Annotation.getMatchQuery` omits each
   471	predicate independently when its value is blank
   472	(`Annotation.java:1205-1209`).
   473	
   474	Phase 2's SQL builds predicates conditionally to match:
   475	
   476	- If `method` is blank, OMIT `AND e."METHOD" = ?`.
   477	- If `methodVersion` is blank, OMIT `AND e."METHODVERSION" = ?`.
   478	
   479	`waitForAnnotationMatchableIds` does the same for the nested OS
   480	clause — omit the corresponding `term` filter when the
   481	respective value is blank. The implementation builds the SQL /
   482	JSON conditionally rather than passing null parameters.
   483	
   484	This is a single `SELECT DISTINCT` query, no materialization of
   485	Annotation objects. Returns a `Set<String>` of annotation IDs.
   486	
   487	#### Phase 3: matchable-visibility wait on the eligible set
   488	
   489	`waitForVisibility` today polls `_count` with an `ids` query
   490	(`OpenSearch.java:521`). That confirms `_id` is in OS but does
   491	NOT confirm the doc has its nested `embeddings.method` /
   492	`methodVersion` indexed — a doc that's been written with the
   493	right `_id` but stale nested fields would pass `_id`-only and
   494	then knn-fail at match time.
   495	
   496	**Scope** (Codex round-3 Major): the helper introduced here is
   497	NOT a full replication of `getMatchingSetQuery` (which adds
   498	taxonomy, viewpoint, iaClass, encounter exclusion, dead-animal
   499	range, task params). It is narrower: "doc has fresh embedding
   500	metadata." That's sufficient for the visibility race the gate
   501	solves — the index lag is between persist and OS write of
   502	embedding metadata, not between persist and the encounter/
   503	taxonomy fields (which are written at the same time as id and
   504	acmId).
   505	
   506	The conceptual `_count` body (matching the shape `queryCount`
   507	expects at `OpenSearch.java:541-552` — uses `ids` query, no
   508	`size`, no `track_total_hits`):
   509	
   510	```json
   511	{
   512	  "query": {
   513	    "bool": {
   514	      "filter": [
   515	        { "ids": { "values": [ ...waitSetIds ] } },
   516	        { "term":  { "matchAgainst": true } },
   517	        { "exists": { "field": "acmId" } },
   518	        { "nested": {
   519	            "path": "embeddings",
   520	            "query": { "bool": { "filter": [
   580	    return GateOutcome.defer(...);
   581	}
   582	```
   583	
   584	This makes the caller's own match resilient: if the caller's
   585	embedding metadata is genuinely missing, that's a different
   586	problem (no candidates returned), not a stuck wait.
   587	
   588	#### Phase 4: run match
   589	
   590	`runMatchProspects(callerAnnotationIds, taskId, matchConfig)` —
   591	**unchanged**. The caller's own annotation IDs drive the match;
   592	the gate only ensures the OS corpus is populated.
   593	
   594	#### Pseudocode
   595	
   596	```java
   597	private MlServiceJobOutcome waitAndRunMatch(List<String> annotationIds,
   598	    String taskId, JSONObject matchConfig, int attempt, Long firstDeferredAt) {
   599	
   600	    GateOutcome g = visibilityGate.gateForBatch(
   601	        annotationIds, taskId, matchConfig, attempt, firstDeferredAt);
   602	    switch (g.kind) {
   603	      case READY:
   604	        return runMatchProspects(annotationIds, taskId, matchConfig);
   605	      case DEFER:
   606	        enqueueDeferredMatch(annotationIds, taskId, matchConfig, g);
   607	        return MlServiceJobOutcome.ok(annotationIds);
   608	      case GIVE_UP:
   609	        // Bounded age-out: run match against whatever is visible
   610	        // rather than block forever. Log WARN + mark task with status
   611	        // detail indicating partial-batch match.
   612	        System.out.println("WARN: MatchVisibilityGate aged out for task "
   613	            + taskId + " after " + (g.attempt) + " attempts ("
   614	            + g.elapsedMillis + "ms): " + g.reason);
   615	        return runMatchProspects(annotationIds, taskId, matchConfig);
   616	    }
   617	}
   618	```
   619	
   620	`MatchVisibilityGate.gateForBatch(callerAnnotationIds, childTaskId,
   621	matchConfig, attempt, firstDeferredAt)` returns `READY` if Phase 1
   622	+ 2 + 3 all succeed, `DEFER` if any phase says "wait", `GIVE_UP`
   623	if `(System.currentTimeMillis() - firstDeferredAt) > MAX_AGE_MS`.
   624	
   625	`callerAnnotationIds` is passed in so the gate can:
   626	- Union it into the visibility set even if the eligibility filter
   627	  would otherwise exclude it (caller's match must not block on its
   628	  own visibility).
   629	- Degrade cleanly when the child task has no parent topTask
   630	  (orphan child case): wait only on callerAnnotationIds.
   631	
   632	#### Deferred-match path (`runDeferredMatch`) and backoff
   633	
   634	Today at `MlServiceProcessor.java:433-442`, `runDeferredMatch`
   635	reads `annotationIds` + `taskId` + `matchConfig` from the job
   636	payload and calls `runMatchProspects` directly — no re-gate.
   637	
   638	The existing requeue mechanism is `IAGateway.requeueJob` at
   639	`IAGateway.java:751-810`. Verified behavior:
   640	
   641	- 30s fixed delay between requeues (line 785: `whileSleepMillis = 30000`).
   642	- Tracks `__queueRetries`, `__queueActualRetries`, `__queueStart`
   643	  on the job JSON.
   644	- Hard caps at `MAX_RETRIES=30` retries OR `MAX_TIME_MILLIS = 2
   645	  days` elapsed.
   646	- mlServiceV2 jobs land back on the detection queue (line 792-797).
   647	
   648	For the deferred-match path we reuse this 30s fixed delay (no
   649	custom backoff). What we add:
   650	
   651	- **`firstDeferredAt`** epoch-ms in the jobData payload, recorded
   652	  on the first DEFER. Used by `gateForBatch` to compute elapsed
   653	  age; `GIVE_UP` fires when elapsed > `MAX_DEFER_AGE_MILLIS`.
   654	- **`attempt`** counter, incremented per DEFER. Logged for
   655	  diagnostics; not the cap (elapsed-time is the cap, per Codex
   656	  round-2 open-question #1).
   657	- **`lastGateReason`** string in jobData, written by `gateForBatch`
   658	  on DEFER. Useful when ops investigate why a match was deferred
   659	  (e.g., "sibling MA 2/4 still processing-mlservice").
   660	
   661	```java
   662	private static final long MAX_DEFER_AGE_MILLIS = 12L * 60L * 1000L;
   663	// 12 minutes from first deferral. Aligns with IAGateway.requeueJob
   664	// real cap (MAX_RETRIES=30 × 30s = ~15min before the queue gives
   665	// up on its own at IAGateway.java:751-810). With 12min, GIVE_UP
   666	// fires inside the queue's window with margin. Most v2 imports
   667	// complete sibling detection in <2min; 12min is 6× normal.
   668	
   669	public MlServiceJobOutcome runDeferredMatch(JSONObject jobData) {
   670	    if (jobData == null) return MlServiceJobOutcome.validationError(...);
   671	    List<String> annotationIds = jsonArrayToStringList(
   672	        jobData.optJSONArray("annotationIds"));
   673	    String taskId = jobData.optString("taskId", null);
   674	    JSONObject matchConfig = jobData.optJSONObject("matchConfig");
   675	    if (matchConfig == null) matchConfig = inferMatchConfig(annotationIds);
   676	    int attempt = jobData.optInt("attempt", 1);
   677	    Long firstDeferredAt = jobData.has("firstDeferredAt")
   678	        ? jobData.optLong("firstDeferredAt") : null;
   679	    // re-gate; deferred match earns the same protection as initial.
   680	    return waitAndRunMatch(annotationIds, taskId, matchConfig,
   681	        attempt, firstDeferredAt);
   682	}
   683	```
   684	
   685	`enqueueDeferredMatch` is extended to take the gate outcome so
   686	it can carry `attempt + 1`, `firstDeferredAt` (set on first call,
   687	preserved on subsequent), and `lastGateReason` into the new
   688	jobData.
   689	
   690	**Use `requeueJob` explicitly** (Codex round-4 Blocker). Setting
   691	`__queueRetries` on the JSON is NOT enough — `IAGateway.requeueJob`
   692	only applies its 30s sleep when called with `increment=true`
   693	(`IAGateway.java:785`). So:
   694	
   695	```java
   696	// In MlServiceProcessor.enqueueDeferredMatch (or its replacement).
   697	private void enqueueDeferredMatch(List<String> annotationIds,
   698	    String taskId, JSONObject matchConfig, GateOutcome lastGate) {
   699	    JSONObject payload = new JSONObject();
   700	    // Routing flags: both are required to reach MlServiceProcessor's
   701	    // deferred entry point. IAGateway dispatches v2 jobs only on
   702	    // `mlServiceV2`; MlServiceProcessor branches into deferred mode
   703	    // on `deferredMatch`. (`mlServiceV2DeferredMatch` is an
   704	    // additional diagnostic marker, not the routing contract.)
   705	    payload.put("mlServiceV2", true);
   706	    payload.put("deferredMatch", true);
   707	    payload.put("mlServiceV2DeferredMatch", true); // diagnostic marker
   708	    payload.put("__context", context);
   709	    payload.put("taskId", taskId);
   710	    payload.put("annotationIds", new JSONArray(annotationIds));
   711	    if (matchConfig != null) payload.put("matchConfig", matchConfig);
   712	    payload.put("attempt", lastGate.attempt + 1);
   713	    payload.put("firstDeferredAt", lastGate.firstDeferredAt);
   714	    payload.put("lastGateReason", lastGate.reason);
   715	    // IAGateway.requeueJob(payload, true) is what creates the 30s
   716	    // delay. Calling addToDetectionQueue directly would fire
   717	    // immediately and hot-loop.
   718	    IAGateway.requeueJob(payload, /* increment= */ true);
   719	}
   720	```
   721	
   722	The age-out at `elapsed > MAX_DEFER_AGE_MILLIS` is a degraded
   723	outcome (matches against whatever is visible) but bounded so a
   724	stuck sibling cannot block the user's match indefinitely.
   725	
   760	
   761	#### Shepherd boundary in `MatchVisibilityGateImpl`
   762	
   763	Explicit phase-locality, per Codex round-2 Major:
   764	
   765	```
   766	Phase 1 + 2 (read DB):
   767	    Shepherd shep = new Shepherd(context);
   768	    shep.setAction(ACTION_PREFIX + "gateForBatch");
   769	    try {
   770	        shep.beginDBTransaction();
   771	        // Phase 1: walk childTaskId → parent topTask → siblings,
   772	        //         read MA.detectionStatus + child Task.status.
   773	        // Phase 2: SELECT DISTINCT eligible annotation IDs (one SQL query).
   774	        // Collect: (allTerminal: boolean, eligibleIds: Set<String>).
   775	        shep.commitDBTransaction();
   776	    } finally {
   777	        shep.rollbackAndClose();
   778	    }
   779	    // Shepherd is now CLOSED.
   780	
   781	Phase 3 (network):
   782	    OpenSearch os = new OpenSearch();
   783	    // Use callerAnnotationIds + eligibleIds for the wait set.
   784	    boolean ok = os.waitForMatchable("annotation",
   785	        union(callerAnnotationIds, eligibleIds),
   786	        method, methodVersion, VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_MS);
   787	```
   788	
   789	No DB transaction is held during the OS poll. Matches the
   790	Phase A/B/C pattern from the WBIA register thread (commit `c6ffe5d20`).
   791	
   792	Real implementation `MatchVisibilityGateImpl` constructs Shepherd +
   793	OpenSearch as needed; takes `context` in constructor.
   794	
   795	`MlServiceProcessor` holds `private final MatchVisibilityGate
   796	visibilityGate`. Default constructor uses
   797	`MatchVisibilityGateImpl`; a package-visible constructor accepts
   798	an injected gate for tests.
   799	
   800	Unit tests can now:
   801	- Stub `gateForBatch` to return READY → assert `runMatchProspects` is
   802	  called with caller's IDs.
   803	- Stub `gateForBatch` to return DEFER → assert `enqueueDeferredMatch`
   804	  is called with attempt counter.
   805	- Stub `gateForBatch` to return GIVE_UP → assert match runs anyway
   806	  (age-out behavior).
   807	- Test the gate implementation separately with a mock OpenSearch
   808	  + Shepherd-test-double.
   809	
   810	Also: extract the JDOQL "find eligible annotations for a topTask"
   811	into a package-visible static method on `Annotation` or a new
   812	`MatchEligibilityQuery` utility, so it can be unit-tested with a
   813	DataNucleus in-memory store (the v2 pattern from c8/c9 tests).
   814	
   815	#### Behavior in edge cases
   816	
   817	- **Child Task has no parent (orphaned single-asset job from
   818	  reconciler)**: gate degrades to "no batch to wait for; just OS
   819	  visibility on caller's IDs". Logs WARN with `childTaskId` (Codex
   820	  round-4 OQ #3): this is an abnormal degraded path, not silent
   821	  normal behavior.
   822	- **Sibling MA permanently stuck in `processing`**: bounded by
   823	  `MAX_DEFER_AGE_MILLIS = 12 min` (well inside `requeueJob`'s
   824	  ~15min queue cap). User's match runs against whatever's
   825	  visible at age-out.
   826	- **All siblings are `error` / `zero_detections`**: Phase 2's
   827	  eligibility filter returns the empty set or only the caller's
   828	  own annotations. `waitForVisibility` on empty set should be
   829	  short-circuit (or no-op); confirm behavior in tests.
   830	- **Two parallel users with overlapping imports**: topTask is
   960	  `Task.getTasksFor(ma)` to return [topTask, oldUnrelatedTask,
   961	  correctChild]; prove gate selects correctChild.
   962	- `gate_usesRawStatusAccessor_notGetStatusShepherd` — verify
   963	  `Task.getStatus(Shepherd)` is NOT called on siblings (mutation
   964	  side-effect would corrupt the gate's read).
   965	- `gate_treatsChildTaskCompleted_asTerminal` — child Task with
   966	  status `"completed"` (Task value) while MA still has status
   967	  `processing-mlservice`; sibling is terminal.
   968	- `waitForAnnotationMatchableIds_usesIdsQuery_notTermsOnUnderscoreId` —
   969	  observed body matches `OpenSearch.java:541` shape.
   970	- `waitForAnnotationMatchableIds_bodyHasNoSizeOrTrackTotal` —
   971	  `_count` doesn't accept those fields cleanly.
   972	- `deferred_agesOutAt12min_withinRequeueCap` — verify GIVE_UP
   973	  fires before requeueJob's 30-retry cap.
   974	- `safeInvalidate_swallowsNull_fromQueryCacheFactory`
   975	- `safeInvalidate_swallowsException_fromInvalidateByName`
   976	
   977	**End-to-end on live deployment:** import 4+ same-species images,
   978	observe that each annotation's `findMatchProspects` result
   979	includes the other 3 as candidates. Same fire-salamander test
   980	case that surfaced this bug.
   981	
   982	## Testability seam (Codex round-4 Medium)
   983	
   984	In addition to the `MatchVisibilityGate` interface, extract a
   985	small publisher interface for the deferred-match enqueue path so
   986	unit tests can assert DEFER cleanly without exposing private
   987	internals or going through `IAGateway.requeueJob` for real:
   988	
   989	```java
   990	package org.ecocean.ia;
   991	
   992	public interface DeferredMatchPublisher {
   993	    void publish(JSONObject payload);
   994	}
   995	
   996	// Real impl wraps IAGateway.requeueJob(payload, true).
   997	// Test impl captures payloads into a list for assertions.
   998	```
   999	
  1000	`MlServiceProcessor` holds `private final DeferredMatchPublisher
  1001	deferredPublisher` and uses it instead of calling
  1002	`IAGateway.requeueJob` directly. Default constructor wires the
  1003	real impl; package-visible constructor accepts a test double.
  1004	
  1005	## Open questions for Codex (round 5 — confirmatory)
  1006	
  1007	All round-2 + round-3 open questions have been resolved and
  1008	incorporated into the design above:
  1009	
  1010	- Cap by elapsed time, not attempt count (round-2 #1).
  1011	- Age-out posture: run match anyway with WARN (round-2 #2).
  1012	- Full v2 terminal-status set, with child Task status checked
  1013	  separately from MA status (round-2 #3, round-3 #3).
  1014	- `skipAutoIndexing`: no special handling (round-2 #4).
  1015	- Direct SQL for eligibility query (round-2 #5).
  1016	- Job payload for attempt + `firstDeferredAt` +
  1017	  `lastGateReason` (round-2 #6).
  1018	- Helper named `waitForAnnotationMatchableIds`, public on

   521	              { "term": { "embeddings.method":        "<method>" } },
   522	              { "term": { "embeddings.methodVersion": "<version>" } }
   523	            ] } }
   524	        } }
   525	      ]
   526	    }
   527	  }
   528	}
   529	```
   530	
   531	If `methodVersion` is null (legacy config without version), omit
   532	the version predicate, matching `getMatchQuery`'s behavior
   533	(strict-when-present, confirmed at `Annotation.java:1205-1209`).
   534	
   535	New method:
   536	
   537	```java
   538	public boolean waitForAnnotationMatchableIds(
   539	    Collection<String> ids,
   540	    String method,
   541	    String methodVersion,
   542	    long timeoutMs)
   543	throws IOException;
   544	```
   545	
   546	Same `_refresh`-on-entry + exponential-backoff polling pattern as
   547	`waitForVisibility`. Lives on `OpenSearch.java` (per Codex round-3
   548	open-question #1: annotation-schema-specific naming makes it
   549	acceptable as a public method). **Empty wait set short-circuits
   550	to `true`** (per round-3 open-question #4) — but normalize after
   551	unioning caller IDs, so the gate always waits on at least the
   552	caller's annotations.
   553	
   554	If `waitForAnnotationMatchableIds` returns false (timeout),
   555	**defer**.
   556	
   557	**Two-wait split** (Codex round-4 Major): passing caller IDs
   558	through the matchable predicate means a caller annotation that's
   559	visible by `_id` but not-yet-matchable would defer until age-out.
   560	Avoid that by running two waits:
   561	
   562	```java
   563	// Caller IDs: weaker predicate (just _id visibility). A caller
   564	// annotation should not block its own match on its own matchable-
   565	// metadata visibility; if it's missing matchAgainst/acmId/
   566	// embedding metadata, the match will just return zero candidates,
   567	// not hang the gate.
   568	if (!os.waitForVisibility("annotation", callerAnnotationIds,
   569	        VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_MS)) {
   570	    return GateOutcome.defer(...);
   571	}
   572	
   573	// Sibling eligible IDs: full matchable predicate (id +
   574	// matchAgainst + acmId + embeddings.method/methodVersion).
   575	Set<String> siblingsOnly = new LinkedHashSet<>(eligibleIds);
   576	siblingsOnly.removeAll(callerAnnotationIds);
   577	if (!siblingsOnly.isEmpty() &&
   578	    !os.waitForAnnotationMatchableIds(siblingsOnly,
   579	        method, methodVersion, VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_MS)) {
   580	    return GateOutcome.defer(...);
   581	}
   582	```
   583	
   584	This makes the caller's own match resilient: if the caller's
   585	embedding metadata is genuinely missing, that's a different
   586	problem (no candidates returned), not a stuck wait.
   587	
   588	#### Phase 4: run match
   589	
   590	`runMatchProspects(callerAnnotationIds, taskId, matchConfig)` —

docs/plans/2026-05-18-empty-match-prospects-codex-review-v5.md:6848:The schema-sensitive part now looks consistent with existing precedent: `ImportTask` already joins `ENCOUNTER_ANNOTATIONS → ANNOTATION_FEATURES → MEDIAASSET_FEATURES` using the same `ID_OID`/`ID_EID` shape, so the proposed eligibility join is not inventing a new mapping. docs/plans/2026-05-18-empty-match-prospects-codex-review-v4.md:6999:docs/plans/2026-05-09-ml-service-migration-v2.md:224:For the partial unique index to actually constrain duplicates, `Annotation.MEDIAASSET_ID_OID` must be populated. The Wildbook context note that `MEDIAASSET_ID_OID` is "not populated in practice" applies to *legacy* annotations created before the Wildbook-owns-acmId era; v2 explicitly sets `ann.setMediaAsset(ma)` in MlServiceProcessor's persistence. For commit #4 to function correctly, every ml-service-created annotation has both `predictModelId` AND `MEDIAASSET_ID_OID` populated; legacy annotations remain unaffected by the partial unique constraint (filtered out by the `WHERE PREDICTMODELID IS NOT NULL` clause).

docs/plans/2026-05-18-empty-match-prospects-codex-review-v4.md:8374:docs/plans/2026-05-09-ml-service-migration-v2.md:224:For the partial unique index to actually constrain duplicates, `Annotation.MEDIAASSET_ID_OID` must be populated. The Wildbook context note that `MEDIAASSET_ID_OID` is "not populated in practice" applies to *legacy* annotations created before the Wildbook-owns-acmId era; v2 explicitly sets `ann.setMediaAsset(ma)` in MlServiceProcessor's persistence. For commit #4 to function correctly, every ml-service-created annotation has both `predictModelId` AND `MEDIAASSET_ID_OID` populated; legacy annotations remain unaffected by the partial unique constraint (filtered out by the `WHERE PREDICTMODELID IS NOT NULL` clause).

docs/plans/2026-05-09-ml-service-migration-v2.md:224:For the partial unique index to actually constrain duplicates, `Annotation.MEDIAASSET_ID_OID` must be populated. The Wildbook context note that `MEDIAASSET_ID_OID` is "not populated in practice" applies to *legacy* annotations created before the Wildbook-owns-acmId era; v2 explicitly sets `ann.setMediaAsset(ma)` in MlServiceProcessor's persistence. For commit #4 to function correctly, every ml-service-created annotation has both `predictModelId` AND `MEDIAASSET_ID_OID` populated; legacy annotations remain unaffected by the partial unique constraint (filtered out by the `WHERE PREDICTMODELID IS NOT NULL` clause).

   776	    public static Map<String, Integer> getAllMediaAssetCounts(Shepherd myShepherd) {
   777	        Query query = myShepherd.getPM().newQuery("javax.jdo.query.SQL",
   778	            "SELECT ie.\"ID_OID\", count(distinct mf.\"ID_OID\") " +
   779	            "FROM \"IMPORTTASK_ENCOUNTERS\" ie " + "JOIN \"ENCOUNTER_ANNOTATIONS\" ea " +
   780	            "ON ie.\"CATALOGNUMBER_EID\" = ea.\"CATALOGNUMBER_OID\" " +
   781	            "JOIN \"ANNOTATION_FEATURES\" af ON ea.\"ID_EID\" = af.\"ID_OID\" " +
   782	            "JOIN \"MEDIAASSET_FEATURES\" mf ON af.\"ID_EID\" = mf.\"ID_EID\" " +
   783	            "GROUP BY ie.\"ID_OID\"");
   784	
   785	        return parseSqlCountResults(query);

    28	public class Task implements java.io.Serializable {
    29	    public static long TIMEOUT_INACTIVE_MILLIS = 7l * 24l * 60l * 60l * 1000l;
    30	    private String id = null;

    36	    private Task parent = null;
    37	    private List<Task> children = null;
    38	    private String parameters = null;
    39	    private String status;
    40	    // general use, but notably will contain error details when status=error
    41	    private String statusDetails = null;
    42	    private Long completionDateInMilliseconds;
    43	    private String queueResumeMessage;
    44	
    45	    public Task() {
    46	        this(Util.generateUUID());
    47	    }
    48	    public Task(String id) {
    49	        this.id = id;
    50	        created = System.currentTimeMillis();
    51	        modified = System.currentTimeMillis();
    52	    }
    53	    // makes a child of the passed Task (and inherits the parameters!!)
    54	    public Task(Task p) {
    55	        this();
    56	        this.setParameters(p.getParameters());
    57	        this.setParent(p);
    58	    }
    59	
    60	    public String getId() {
    61	        return id;
    62	    }
    63	
    64	    public long getCreatedLong() {
    65	        return created;
    66	    }
    67	
    68	    public long getModifiedLong() {
    69	        return modified;
    70	    }
   285	        return (children == null) ? 0 : children.size();

   288	    public boolean hasChildren() {
   289	        return (this.numChildren() > 0);
   290	    }
   291	
   292	    // omg i am going to assume no looping
   293	    public List<Task> getLeafTasks() {
   294	        List<Task> leaves = new ArrayList<Task>();
   295	
   296	        if (!this.hasChildren()) {
   297	            leaves.add(this);
   298	            return leaves;
   299	        }
   300	        for (Task kid : children) {
   301	            leaves.addAll(kid.getLeafTasks());
   302	        }
   303	        return leaves;
   304	    }
   305	
   306	    public Task getRootTask() {
   307	        if (parent == null) return this;
   308	        return parent.getRootTask();
   309	    }
   310	
   311	    public Task deepContains(Annotation ann) {
   312	        if (this.contains(ann)) return this;
   313	        if (!this.hasChildren()) return null;
   314	        for (Task kid : children) {
   315	            Task found = kid.deepContains(ann);
   316	            if (found != null) return found;
   317	        }
   318	        return null;
   319	    }
   320	
   321	    public Task deepContains(MediaAsset ma) {
   322	        if (this.contains(ma)) return this;
   323	        if (!this.hasChildren()) return null;
   324	        for (Task kid : children) {
   325	            Task found = kid.deepContains(ma);
   326	            if (found != null) return found;
   327	        }
   328	        return null;
   329	    }
